mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and residential investment, which reduces aggregate economic activity and forces equity valuations lower over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day S&P 500 returns with an out-of-sample accuracy exceeding 52% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month rebounds in the industrial production index following a prior contraction signal rapid supply-side recovery and improved manufacturing utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO rebounds during post-shock recovery periods yield negative or flat 5-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 4.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that tightens financial conditions and increases equity discount rates, systematically producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds the 4.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year correlation between PAYEMS growth and 60-day S&P 500 returns falls below 0.05, or positive returns occur in less than 45% of growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP coinciding with a monthly decline in retail sales confirms a broad-based demand shock that immediately triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent five trading days.
failure_condition: Concurrent GDPC1 contraction and RSAFS decline fails to predict negative 5-day S&P 500 returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during mid-cycle expansions signals improving capacity utilization and rising corporate profit expectations, which systematically drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration during mid-cycle expansions yields negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observed instances.
